document.title: Post-Market Surveillance Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council
MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8 Guidelines on a medical devices vigilance system (January 2013)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post-market surveillance plan for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines PMS data collection, risk assessment, trend reporting, competent authority communication, traceability, PMCF, and PSUR update/reporting processes for the device.
keyPoints: PMS data sources include sales volume, feedback and complaints, scientific literature, regulatory databases, similar device information, and product quality information.
Risk assessment uses five-stage severity and probability scales and references risk management plan and report documents.
Trend reports are uploaded to EUDAMED and the notified body is informed when likelihood and severity exceed previously estimated values.
The product is stated to be Class IIb, with PSUR updated at least annually and reported when required by the notified body.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance plan
concepts.evidence: The post-market surveillance system referred to in Article 83 shall be based on this post-market surveillance plan

concepts.name: Periodic Safety Update Report
concepts.evidence: The below information will be reported in PSUR refer to Section 15 of this document.

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-PMCFP-5-001 Post-market Clinical Follow-up Plan.

concepts.name: Trend report
concepts.evidence: Trend report focus on non-serious incidents and undesirable side effects.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: perform benefit-risk analysis and reassessment of risk management when certain indicators reach their respective thresholds.

concepts.name: Traceability
concepts.evidence: Manufacturing date, Lot no., Ref. no., quantity and place of shipment are recorded to ensure traceability.

concepts.name: UDI system
concepts.evidence: Later, when the UDI system is started, traceability is guaranteed through UDI system.

evidence.claim: The document is A-PMS-5-001, revision 2, dated March 8, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-PMS-5-001 Rev. No. 2 Post-market Surveillance Plan Rev. Date March 8, 2024

evidence.claim: The plan applies to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: Revision 2 was issued as a response to the 2nd review by 3EC (NB).
evidence.evidenceText: 2 March 8, 2024 Response to 2nd review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: The PMS plan references Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8.
evidence.evidenceText: Related References • Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council • MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8 Guidelines on a medical devices vigilance system (January 2013)

evidence.claim: The detectors are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and child human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: PMS information collected for PSUR includes sales volume, complaints, literature review, regulatory database searches, similar device information, and product quality information.
evidence.evidenceText: The below information will be reported in PSUR refer to Section 15 of this document. 5.1 Volume of Sales ... 5.2 Feedbacks and complaints ... 5.3 Scientific Literature Review ... 5.4 Public Databased and/or Registry Data ... 5.5 Publicly available information about similar medical devices ... 5.6 Product quality information

evidence.claim: Regulatory database searches include FDA Recall, MFDS recall and suspension of sale, and MHRA alerts and recalls.
evidence.evidenceText: Search via the databases of regulatory authorities • FDA Recall ... • MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sale (Korea) ... • MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices (UK)

evidence.claim: PMS data are analyzed in the PSUR for new risks related to performance and safety of the company's devices or similar devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The data from PMS are analysed whether there are new risks related to the performance and safety of our devices or similar devices in PSUR

evidence.claim: Risk assessment divides severity and probability into five stages.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk assessment divides severity and probability into 5 stages, records them in the risk management report, and manages them according to risk management procedures.

evidence.claim: The user complaint threshold value for physical damage is one time per year.
evidence.evidenceText: User Complaint Threshold Values: 1 time / 1 year

evidence.claim: Trend reports focus on non-serious incidents and undesirable side effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Trend report focus on non-serious incidents and undesirable side effects.

evidence.claim: If analyzed likelihood and severity exceed previous estimates, risk management and CAPA are implemented and the trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED and the notified body is informed.
evidence.evidenceText: If the analysed likelihood and severity are higher than the previously estimated values, risk management and CAPA are implemented and trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED and inform NB.

evidence.claim: Information including serious accidents and FSCA, periodic summary reports, trend reports, PSUR, and FSN is reported or managed through EUDAMED.
evidence.evidenceText: The following information is reported/managed through the electronic system (EUDAMED). (1) Report by manufacturer on serious accidents and FSCA (2) Periodic summary reports by the manufacturer (3) Manufacturer’s summary report on trend (Trend Report) (4) PSUR (Class III and Class IIb implantable products) (5) FSN by the manufacturer

evidence.claim: The EU representative is JaviTech e.K in Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany.
evidence.evidenceText: EU representative JaviTech e.K Sachsenhausener Straße 16, 65824 Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany

evidence.claim: The notified body is 3EC International a.s., notified body number 2265.
evidence.evidenceText: Notified Body 3EC International a.s. Notified Body number: 2265

evidence.claim: The PMS plan is prepared according to Articles 83, 84, and 86 of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: We have prepared the procedures for Post Market Surveillance system according to Article 83 ... A-PMS-5-001 Post-market Surveillance Plan is prepared according to Article 84 ... and Article 86 ... of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: Traceability records include manufacturing date, lot number, reference number, quantity, and place of shipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing date, Lot no., Ref. no., quantity and place of shipment are recorded to ensure traceability.

evidence.claim: The PMCF plan specifies methods and procedures to verify clinical performance and safety, acceptability of identified risks, and detect emerging risks.
evidence.evidenceText: To verify the clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of our medical device, the acceptability of identified risks, and to detect emerging risks based on factual evidence, we specify the methods and procedures of PMCF activities in A-PMCFP-5-001.

evidence.claim: The product is Class IIb and the PSUR will be updated at least once a year and reported when the certification authority requires it.
evidence.evidenceText: Our product is Class IIb, so we will update the PSUR at least once a year and report the PSUR when the certification authority requires it.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document primarily addresses Regulation (EU) 2017/745, EUDAMED, competent authorities, EU representative, and notified body; FDA appears only as a recall database source.
The term certification authority is used for PSUR reporting, but the table states reporting is required by the notified body for Class IIb and Class III.
